name: Food Preservation
tagline: Drying, salting, smoking, fermenting, and storing calories so labour is not wasted on repeated hunting and gathering
problem:
Fresh food spoils on a clock measured in hours for meat, days for many vegetables, and weeks at best for dry grain unless protected from moisture and pests. A civilisation cannot store labour — every calorie someone hunts, digs, or harvests must either be eaten soon, preserved, or lost. Loss is not merely hunger tomorrow; it is the inability to fund specialists who do anything other than finding the next meal.

Preservation is also a public-health problem. Clostridium botulinum and other anaerobes love sealed jars of low-acid food; salt concentration must be high enough in brines; pH must be low enough in ferments; smoke deposits must actually carry antimicrobial compounds, not merely smell nice. Ignorance does not produce mild food — it produces corpses.

Geography changes what works: humid tropics fight drying; cold dry winters favour freeze-drying on racks; coasts offer salt; inland peoples lean on smoke, fermentation, and tight grain discipline. Any guide that pretends one recipe fits all climates is dangerous.

Finally, preservation interacts with justice: who controls the granary controls survival. Building transparent rules for weighing, sealing, and distributing stored food is political technology wrapped around chemistry.
overview:
Heat preservation removes water or kills surface microbes. Sun drying and wind drying work when relative humidity cooperates; thin slices and good airflow beat thick slabs left in shade. Smoking combines heat with deposited phenols and other compounds that inhibit bacterial growth on meat surfaces — the craft is draft, temperature, and time, not colour alone.

Salt and sugar osmotically bind water away from microbes; heavy brines preserve fish and meat for months if kept cool enough that fat does not go rancid. Fat sealing — confit — uses anaerobic layers of rendered fat over cooked meat as a barrier when cellar temperatures stay low.

Fermentation partners with desirable microbes: lactobacilli acidify vegetables, dropping pH below the comfort zone of pathogens while producing vitamins and digestibility gains. The art is cleanliness in the first days, salt level, and exclusion of oxygen where moulds would win.

Cold storage without electricity uses earth coupling: root cellars, ice houses, and deep pits leverage stable ground temperatures. Rodent-proofing with tight doors and raised platforms is non-negotiable — a mouse can undo a winter of potatoes in a week.

Calorie accounting is simple arithmetic: preserved inventory equals deferred labour. A month of grain for ten adults is civilisation-level slack; without it, every rainstorm is a crisis.

principles.name: Water activity
principles.explanation: Microbes need available moisture; drying, salt, and sugar lower water activity.

principles.name: pH and anaerobia
principles.explanation: Acid and salt select microbial communities; sealed low-acid jars are botulism risk without modern pressure canning.

principles.name: Smoke chemistry
principles.explanation: Wood species and smoulder temperature change deposited compounds; creosote-heavy smoke tastes bitter and can be unhealthy in excess.

principles.name: Rodent exclusion
principles.explanation: Stored calories attract competitors; physical barriers beat poison in collapse ethics.

components.name: Drying rack
components.function: Exposes thin food layers to sun and wind

components.name: Smoke chamber
components.function: Deposits antimicrobial smoke while gently heating meat
components.madeFrom: Earth trench, barrel, or timber box

components.name: Brine vat
components.function: High-salt solution for fish and meat
components.madeFrom: Ceramic or wood lined with pitch if needed
components.criticalNote: Weigh salt per water volume; taste is a poor meter

components.name: Root cellar
components.madeFrom: Stone walls, vent pipes, insulated hatch

components.name: Fat seal or wax cap
components.criticalNote: Break seal only with clean tools

rawMaterials.name: Salt
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Imported terrestrial salt until brine evaporation or electrolysis by-products exist in situ
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Dissolve and filter brine if gritty; measure density for reliable fish cures

rawMaterials.name: Wood fuel
rawMaterials.purpose: Heat for drying and smoke generation
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Electric dehydrators or resistive ovens in habitats; no open combustion in oxygen-limited modules
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Season wood; split small for controlled smoulder

rawMaterials.name: Food-grade vessels
rawMaterials.spaceAlternatives: Stainless vessels with microbially clean welds
rawMaterials.processingRequired: Glaze food-safe; boil new crocks before first acid fill

buildSteps.title: Survey climate and pick two primary methods
buildSteps.description: If humidity is high, plan smoking and fermentation before sun-only drying. If winters are hard, prioritise cellar and freezing on racks.
buildSteps.warningNote: Open flame near drying meat ignites fat drips — keep drip trays and clearance

buildSteps.title: Build drying infrastructure before harvest peak
buildSteps.description: Lattice racks, bird netting, and cordage take days. Test load with inedible sacrificial slices to learn airflow.
buildSteps.warningNote: Falls from roof-mounted racks are common — ladder discipline

buildSteps.title: Run a measured brine trial on small fish
buildSteps.description: Record salt mass, water volume, and soak time. Taste for saltiness after soak; adjust before scaling.
buildSteps.warningNote: Under-salted fish can host Clostridium botulinum in sealed jars — never home-can low-acid foods like modern pressure canners without equivalent equipment

buildSteps.title: Smoke a training batch with thermometry
buildSteps.description: Hold chamber between roughly 50–80 °C for long smokes depending on recipe tradition; vent smoke away from sleeping areas.
buildSteps.warningNote: Carbon monoxide from smouldering fires kills in enclosed sheds — ventilate

buildSteps.title: Rodent-proof the store
buildSteps.description: Stone thresholds, metal flashing, tight doors, and cat discipline. Inspect weekly with lamps for droppings.
buildSteps.warningNote: Rat bites transmit disease — wear gloves during cleanup

history.event: Granaries and salting enable cities and standing armies.
Freeze drying and controlled atmosphere storage reshape supply chains.

impact:
Preserved food turns seasonal glut into political stability: tax grain, feed garrisons, survive siege. Without it, every society lives hand-to-mouth literally.

Space colonies depend on the same mathematics with better seals.
